title: Neottia morrisonicola
intro: Neottia morrisonicola, also known as the Morrison's lip orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. Traditionally, Neottia morrisonicola is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Neottia morrisonicola include iridoids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neottia morrisonicola is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to make teas, tinctures, and tablets for the treatment of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Neottia morrisonicola is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to Taiwan and is classified under the genus Neottia, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. The family Orchidaceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species. Neottia morrisonicola is further classified as a member of the subtribe Neottieae, which includes many species of terrestrial orchids. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Neottia morrisonicola and other related species.
